steps.title: Identify the claim to support
steps.content: Patel and colleagues claim that caterpillar sounds are directed primarily at predators. The strongest support should show predators noticing those sounds and changing their behavior in response.

steps.title: Look for predator response
steps.content: Choice A supports only some detectability and excludes whistles. Choice B describes variation within species, and Choice D describes differences among species; neither shows predator-directed function. Evidence that predators stop attacks after hearing the sounds directly supports the claim. The answer is C.
